title: English as a Second Language * Hispanic/Latino Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > English as a Second Language * Hispanic/Latino Community 10 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Day Laborers Hispanic/Latino Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic education program for Latina women working towards a GED, including coursework in English Language Development (ELD), mathematics, grammar, parenting, and an 18-week seminar focused on conflict resolution (ESPERE). The Early Childhood Education program provides preschool and child care for participants' children. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Low-income Spanish-speaking women and Also offers help with resumes, job applications, small business workshops, and related advice. 185 NW 4th Street Suite 1 Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-5pm or by appointment, Thursday 10am-7pm Email (541) 788-4829 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION REDMOND WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 636 NW Cedar Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 316-5456 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES ADULT EDUCATION CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers adult education programs including: English as a Second Languages classes, Oregon Drivers License exam, Citizenship exam, and computer literacy classes. 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Varies by service (541) 686-8483 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES YOUTH & FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational classes, including English as a Second Language (ESL) and literacy classes. After school and summer youth enrichment programs with a focus on connecting with community resources, hearing from presenters on topics of interest, engaging in volunteer service activities, and gaining skills important for the future, such as resume building, scholarship writing, job searching, personal financial management, and navigating the health system. Child care is provided for ESL and literacy classes 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies with a primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Classes and youth enrichment: No standard hours Phone: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM MEDFORD LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that provides adult English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction and adult basic education for parents. Children participate in the program with their parents by attending developmentally appropriate classes and getting homework help and Spanish reading support. 1001 Beall Lane Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-15 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 5:30pm-8pm (541) 734-5150 1049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY ESL LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD ADULT EDUCATION CLASSES SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM
